patch.value: MCP pm_search defaults to full item bodies, blowing past agent token limits

Through the pm-mcp server, pm_search returned no projection by default and fell back to mode=full (entire item bodies/comments/notes per hit). A 10-hit hybrid query produced ~175KB of output that exceeded the agent tool token cap and had to be spilled to disk — making search nearly unusable for agents and wasting context. pm_list (pm-2cqx) and pm_get/pm_context already default to low-token projections; pm_search was the remaining gap.

MCP pm_search defaults to a compact projection (id/title/status/type/priority/updated_at/score/matched_fields) when the caller passes no compact/full/fields option; callers can still opt into full via options.full=true; tool description documents the default and opt-outs; regression test asserts compact-by-default and full opt-in through handleRequest.

value.note: search action injects compact default + documents projection opt-outs
asserts pm_search compact-by-default + full opt-in via handleRequest

patch.value: Fixed in src/mcp/server.ts: the search case now injects compact=true when the caller passes no compact/full/fields option (mirroring the pm_list pattern), and the pm_search tool description documents the compact default plus options.mode/limit/fields/full opt-outs. Added regression test in tests/integration/mcp-dynamic-package-actions.spec.ts asserting compact-by-default (flat hit, no body/item nesting) and full opt-in (nested item) through handleRequest. Build green; MCP integration spec passes 4/4. NOTE: the long-running pm-mcp server process in this session still serves the previous build, so a fresh full-bodied response is expected until the MCP server restarts — verified the new behavior via the rebuilt CLI/handleRequest path.
MCP pm_search now defaults to a compact projection; full bodies are opt-in via options.full=true.
Agent search calls return token-efficient compact hits by default without exceeding tool output caps.
Default MCP pm_search returns flat compact hits (id/title/status/type/priority/updated_at/score/matched_fields); options.full=true returns nested full items. Regression test passes 4/4.
